headline: XLY ETF Holdings List — AMEX:XLY
summary: This article provides a detailed list of the top holdings for the XLY ETF (State Street Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF) as of February 27, 2026. It includes the weight percentage, number of shares, and market value for each company held within the ETF. Key holdings include Amazon.com, Inc., Tesla, Inc., Home Depot, Inc., and McDonald's Corporation, among others.
label: positive

headline: Despite environmental concerns, Stockton City Council enters 20-year partnership with Home Depot
summary: The Stockton City Council approved a 20-year partnership with The Home Depot, projecting $500,000 to $1 million in annual revenue for the city. This agreement will facilitate the expansion of Home Depot's e-commerce and distribution operations in a new 600,000 to 700,000 square foot facility at the Port of Stockton, creating 80-100 permanent jobs. Despite environmental concerns raised by the Delta-Sierra Group regarding the health impacts on nearby residents from increased emissions, the council voted 6-1 in favor, with Mayor Christina Fugazi noting the facility is already near completion and the potential revenue is crucial for community reinvestment.
label: negative

headline: Home Depot nears completion on South Hill
summary: Home Depot's new South Hill store, located at 4515 S. Regal in Spokane, is nearing completion with an expected opening in June. The $17.8 million project includes a 108,000-square-foot building and a 26,000-square-foot garden center, creating nearly 200 jobs. This new development comes after a previous plan in 2008 was canceled due to neighborhood objections.
label: negative

headline: News | Intermodal transportation network, population growth convince Home Depot to expand in Edmonton
summary: The Home Depot has expanded its distribution network in Edmonton, Alberta, with a new 206,000-square-foot facility developed by Qualico Properties. This expansion, prompted by Edmonton's intermodal transportation network and population growth, supports Home Depot's 11 stores in the region. The project, completed in two phases for $36.9 million, earned a 2025 CoStar Impact Award for commercial development for Edmonton.
label: negative

headline: Home Depot, world's largest home improvement chain, plans to build its fifth Polk County store
summary: Home Depot is planning to build its fifth store in Polk County, a 135,632-square-foot retail store in Davenport on U.S. 27. The project will include a 28,000-square-foot garden center and will be reviewed by Polk County's Development Review Committee on March 27. This new location will provide jobs and serve the growing Davenport area, which is seeing numerous new housing and commercial developments.
label: negative
